summary: Elevated BMI is associated with an increased risk of complications such as cardiac issues, metabolic disorders, and poor overall survival due to its correlation with chronic inflammatory states and adverse health conditions common among obese individuals. This relationship persists regardless of specific tumor characteristics or treatment modalities post-gastrectomy.
